key_insight: TO-Master uses an LLM agent to turn topology optimization into a conversational, tool-driven workflow that automates model setup, solver selection, and optimization execution.
engineering_relevance: It lowers the expert burden for finite-element-based topology optimization, making compliance, thermal, and stress-constrained design studies more accessible to non-specialists.
method: Natural-language instructions are parsed into FE topology-optimization tasks with optional mesh, geometry, or image inputs.
The agent orchestrates deterministic tools for mesh checking, boundary-condition assignment, solver setup, and sensitivity-based optimization.
Supports 2D/3D compliance, thermal conduction, multiple load cases, stress constraints, and engineering geometries.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports benchmark reproduction and more complex engineering examples, with outputs including optimized designs, field plots, convergence histories, and interactive artifacts.
watch_item: Performance depends on correct tool-usage rules and prompt guidance; ambiguous inputs can still lead to fragile formulation or setup errors.
paper_title: TO-Master: an LLM-agent framework for automated topology optimization
